suggestion_reasons.Causal Inter-Paragraph Dependencies: Scoring at maximum differentiation (5 vs. 2 vs. 3) and confirmed by the user as the non-negotiable preference — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Motivation-Before-Mechanism: Scoring at maximum differentiation (5 vs. 2 vs. 3) and the rubric-guided draft outperforms both alternatives — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Explicit Argumentative Payoff: Scoring at maximum differentiation (5 vs. 2 vs. 3) and the rubric-guided draft's contrastive closing is exactly what the user praised — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Construct-Before-Measurement Priority: The user explicitly ranked the rubric-guided draft first because it 'earns the construct definition by explaining why construct definition has to come first before metrics can mean anything' — this is a distinct move from Motivation-Before-Mechanism (which covers design choices) and not captured by any existing criterion. Added to formalize the requirement that when a section introduces a measurement or evaluation framework, the construct being measured must be defined and justified before any metric is named, with the definition itself motivated by what goes wrong without it.

suggestion_reasons.Causal Inter-Paragraph Dependencies: Kept unchanged — this criterion is correctly differentiating, and the user's praise for the original preference draft ('dependency logic is tight: it moves from progress → structural limitation → gap, and each clause earns the next') confirms the criterion captures real value; the rubric-guided draft scored highest here, validating the criterion as written.
suggestion_reasons.Motivation-Before-Mechanism: Kept unchanged — all three drafts scored reasonably on this criterion and the user's comments don't identify it as a failure point; the rubric-guided draft scored highest, so no rewording is needed.
suggestion_reasons.Construct-Before-Measurement Priority: Kept unchanged — this criterion applies primarily to evaluation subsections rather than Related Work openings, so the scoring variance across all three drafts was low and reflects context rather than a criterion weakness.
suggestion_reasons.Explicit Argumentative Payoff: Reworded the description and added a dimension to capture what the user actually valued in the original preference draft's closing — economy and dependency rather than just contrastive justification. The user criticized the generic and rubric-guided drafts' closings as either restating the gap or producing an exhausting final sentence, while praising the original preference draft's 'real economy'; the criterion needs to explicitly reward tight, dependency-creating closings, not just named advantages relative to alternatives.
suggestion_reasons.Strategic Plain-Language Clarity: Reworded to de-emphasize plain-language as the primary vehicle of clarity and instead foreground conceptual precision — the user praised 'accountable to them' and the 'stable signal vs. contextually sensitive' framing as the sharpest moves, neither of which is plain-language in the colloquial sense but both of which demonstrate mastery through exact conceptual distinction. The existing criterion risks rewarding simplification over precision.
suggestion_reasons.Terminological Precision and Consistency: Kept unchanged — the rubric-guided draft scored highest here and the user's comments don't identify this as a failure point; criterion is functioning as intended.
suggestion_reasons.Formal Register Without Camouflage: Added a dimension explicitly flagging rhetorical or essayistic opening moves as register violations, because the user's primary objection to the rubric-guided draft was its opening ('harder than it sounds is too casual for a methods-adjacent section') — the existing criterion's dimensions do not cover the specific failure mode of casualness introduced through rhetorical framing rather than vocabulary.
suggestion_reasons.Citation-Aware Positioning: Added this new criterion because the user explicitly cited citation placeholders as a reason to prefer the original preference draft ('signals it's genuinely cite-aware, not just gesturing at the literature'), and no existing criterion covers the expectation that related work openings position claims relative to specific, citable prior work rather than unnamed abstractions — this is a genuine structural gap the existing rubric cannot address through rewording alone.

suggestion_reasons.Causal Inter-Unit Dependencies: Kept unchanged — the rubric-guided draft scored 5 here and the user confirmed the dependency logic was tight, so this criterion is working exactly as intended.
suggestion_reasons.Motivation-Before-Mechanism: Kept unchanged — the rubric-guided draft scored 5 and the user's comments about the 'motivating question, approach, formal detail, payoff' rhythm confirm this criterion is correctly capturing what they want.
suggestion_reasons.Construct-Before-Measurement Priority: Kept unchanged — the rubric-guided draft outscored both alternatives, and this criterion addresses a structural need the user hasn't flagged as broken; no evidence supports modifying it.
suggestion_reasons.Explicit Argumentative Payoff: The user's complaint that the original preference draft 'front-loads the conclusion before earning it' and 'wraps up too neatly without structural tension' reveals a gap in the current criterion: payoffs must feel structurally necessary, not just stated with stakes. Added a new dimension requiring that the closing sentence creates forward pressure or unresolved tension where appropriate, rather than achieving clean resolution that deflates the section.
suggestion_reasons.Strategic Plain-Language Clarity: The user's specific praise — 'used only once, which is the rare strategic informality I find genuinely effective' — signals that restraint in deployment is as important as placement. Added a dimension requiring that crystallizing sentences are used sparingly so each occurrence retains force, since overuse was implicitly flagged as a weakness of the rubric-guided draft relative to the original preference draft.
suggestion_reasons.Terminological Precision and Consistency: Kept unchanged — the rubric-guided draft scored highest and the user made no complaints traceable to terminological inconsistency.
suggestion_reasons.Formal Register Without Camouflage: Kept unchanged — the rubric-guided draft scored 5 and the user's feedback on the generic draft's register failures ('vague motivation masquerading as narrative') confirms this criterion is correctly calibrated.
suggestion_reasons.Citation-Aware Positioning: Kept unchanged — the rubric-guided draft scored highest and the user's feedback did not reference citation handling as a concern in this passage.

suggestion_reasons.Motivation-Before-Mechanism: Scoring perfectly on the rubric-guided draft and confirmed by the user as the defining strength — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Causal Inter-Unit Dependencies: The rubric-guided draft scored 5 and the user explicitly praised the logical rather than sequential dependency between paragraphs — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Terminological Precision and Consistency: Scoring well and differentiating clearly across all three drafts — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Explicit Argumentative Payoff: Scoring perfectly and the user called out the payoff sentence as earned and illuminating — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Strategic Plain-Language Clarity: Scoring perfectly and the user singled out the 'The key insight is...' moment as used exactly once and genuinely clarifying — kept exactly as-is.
suggestion_reasons.Construct-Before-Measurement Priority: The scorer noted this criterion is less directly applicable to representation-introduction sections, and all three drafts clustered at 3–4 with limited differentiation; narrowing the description to clarify it applies only when evaluation or measurement frameworks are explicitly introduced prevents it from generating weak or inapplicable scores in sections where no measurement apparatus appears.
suggestion_reasons.Formal Register Without Camouflage: Scoring consistently and differentiating at least modestly — kept exactly as-is.
